message:
Add summary/coordinator/cass pure function branch tests (bd-24ljk)

summary: formatDetailed/formatBrief/formatHandoff/appendPromptList/
appendPromptFiles/cleanContextLine/parseBulletItem → 88.1% to 92.4%

coordinator: computeConfidence/selectDependency/buildAssignmentReason →
100%, estimateTaskComplexity → 95%, overall 71.4% to 72.3%

cass: FlexTime.UnmarshalJSON float path/formatMarkdown age branch →
100%, overall 71.1% to 72.1%

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24xlg","title":"Unit Tests: ntm errors command pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Unit tests for the ntm errors command covering error pattern matching, time filtering, and JSON output.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/cmd/errors_test.go
package cmd

func TestErrors_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        line     string
        isError  bool
        matchType string
    }{
        // Python errors
        {"python_traceback", "Traceback (most recent call last):", true, "traceback"},
        {"python_exception", "FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file", true, "exception"},

        // Go errors
        {"go_panic", "panic: runtime error: index out of range", true, "panic"},
        {"go_error", "error: cannot find package", true, "error"},

        // JavaScript errors
        {"js_error", "Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory", true, "error"},
        {"js_type_error", "TypeError: Cannot read property 'x' of undefined", true, "exception"},

        // Generic patterns
        {"failed_lower", "build failed: compilation error", true, "failed"},
        {"failed_upper", "FAILED: unit tests", true, "failed"},
        {"exit_code", "Process exited with code 1", true, "exit"},

        // Non-errors
        {"normal_output", "Building project...", false, ""},
        {"info_message", "INFO: Starting server", false, ""},
        {"success", "All tests passed", false, ""},
    }

    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Line: %q", tt.line)

            match, matchType := isErrorLine(tt.line)

            t.Logf("[TEST] isError=%v, matchType=%s", match, matchType)

            assert.Equal(t, tt.isError, match)
            if tt.isError {
                assert.NotEmpty(t, matchType)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestErrors_TimeFiltering(t *testing.T) {
    now := time.Now()

    errors := []ErrorEntry{
        {Timestamp: now.Add(-1 * time.Minute), Line: "Error 1"},
        {Timestamp: now.Add(-5 * time.Minute), Line: "Error 2"},
        {Timestamp: now.Add(-15 * time.Minute), Line: "Error 3"},
        {Timestamp: now.Add(-60 * time.Minute), Line: "Error 4"},
    }

    tests := []struct {
        since    time.Duration
        expected int
    }{
        {2 * time.Minute, 1},
        {10 * time.Minute, 2},
        {30 * time.Minute, 3},
        {2 * time.Hour, 4},
    }

    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.since.String(), func(t *testing.T) {
            filtered := filterBySince(errors, tt.since)
            t.Logf("[TEST] since=%v, expected=%d, got=%d", tt.since, tt.expected, len(filtered))
            assert.Len(t, filtered, tt.expected)
        })
    }
}

func TestErrors_JSONOutput(t *testing.T) {
    result := ErrorsResult{
        Session: "my_session",
        Errors: []ErrorEntry{
            {Pane: 2, Timestamp: time.Now(), Line: "Error: test"},
        },
        TotalErrors: 1,
    }

    data, err := json.Marshal(result)
    require.NoError(t, err)

    t.Logf("[TEST] JSON output: %s", string(data))

    assert.Contains(t, string(data), "my_session")
    assert.Contains(t, string(data), "total_errors")
}

func TestErrors_ContextLines(t *testing.T) {
    lines := []string{
        "line 1: normal",
        "line 2: normal",
        "line 3: Error: something failed",
        "line 4: normal",
        "line 5: normal",
    }

    result := extractWithContext(lines, 2, 2, 2)

    t.Logf("[TEST] Context extracted: %v", result)

    // Should include 2 lines before and 2 after
    assert.Len(t, result.Context, 5)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test all error pattern types (traceback, exception, panic, error, failed)
- [ ] Test time filtering with --since
- [ ] Test pane filtering with --panes
- [ ] Test JSON output structure
- [ ] Test context line extraction
- [ ] Detailed test logging with [TEST] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:32.943900770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:09:28.557922451Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:09:28.557840486Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24xlg","depends_on_id":"bd-38x1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-254do","title":"Task: Implement RedundancyScore for output similarity detection","description":"# Task: RedundancyScore Implementation

Detect how similar outputs are (high = wasteful).

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/similarity.go
type RedundancyAnalysis struct {
    OverallScore    float64 // 0-1, higher = more redundant
    PairwiseScores  []PairSimilarity
    Recommendations []string
}

type PairSimilarity struct {
    ModeA        string
    ModeB        string
    Similarity   float64 // 0-1
    SharedFindings int
    UniqueToA    int
    UniqueToB    int
}

func CalculateRedundancy(outputs []ModeOutput) *RedundancyAnalysis
func (r *RedundancyAnalysis) GetHighRedundancyPairs(threshold float64) []PairSimilarity
func (r *RedundancyAnalysis) SuggestReplacements(catalog *ModeCatalog) []string
func (r *RedundancyAnalysis) Render() string
```

## Similarity Calculation (Deterministic)
1. Build normalized finding sets per mode:
   - normalize finding text + evidence_pointer when present
2. Jaccard similarity on findings sets
3. Optional small weight for recommendation overlap (normalized text)
4. Combine with fixed weights (e.g., 0.8 findings, 0.2 recommendations)

No heavy NLP or external models.

## Visualization
```
Redundancy Analysis:
Overall Score: 0.34 (acceptable)

Pairwise Similarity:
F1 ↔ E2: 0.23 (low - good diversity)
F1 ↔ K1: 0.67 (HIGH - overlapping insights)
E2 ↔ L3: 0.18 (low - good diversity)

Recommendation: Consider replacing K1 with different mode
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log top-N redundant pairs with similarity
- Log recommendation inputs (mode IDs, overlap counts)

## Tests
- Unit: exact match → similarity 1.0
- Unit: disjoint findings → similarity 0.0
- Unit: partial overlap → expected Jaccard
- Unit: recommendation-weighted effect (if enabled)

## File: internal/ensemble/similarity.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:44:21.023982336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:43:55.723914025Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:43:55.723786885Z","close_reason":"Implemented RedundancyScore with pairwise Jaccard similarity, weighted findings/recommendations, tests, and Render() visualization","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-254do","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-254do","depends_on_id":"bd-397f0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-308f5","title":"rano Integration: Network Observer for Per-Agent API Tracking","description":"Integrate rano for network-level visibility into agent API calls.

## Project Location
/dp/rano (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

When agents are working:
- Which agent is making API calls?
- What is the latency per call?
- How much data is being transferred?
- Which provider is being called?

Currently invisible without rano.

## What rano Does

rano monitors network activity per process:

### Data Collection
- Reads /proc/net/tcp, /proc/net/tcp6
- Correlates connections to PIDs via /proc/<pid>/fd
- Tracks bytes sent/received per connection
- Attributes to known providers (api.anthropic.com, api.openai.com, etc.)

### Key rano Commands
```bash
rano status              # Current connections
rano status --pid 1234   # Connections for specific PID
rano history --today     # Historical data
rano watch               # Real-time monitoring
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. PID Mapping
NTM knows which PID belongs to which pane via tmux session → pane → shell PID.

### 2. Dashboard Widget
Show per-agent network activity.

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-rano-stats: Get network stats per agent

## Acceptance Criteria

1. rano binary detected and version checked
2. PID-to-pane mapping implemented
3. Per-agent traffic attribution works
4. Provider breakdown accurate
5. Config section [integrations.rano] works
6. Graceful fallback when rano missing
7. Unit tests for detection and attribution (80% coverage)
8. E2E test for full rano integration","acceptance_criteria":"1. rano binary detected and version checked
2. PID-to-pane mapping implemented
3. Per-agent traffic attribution works
4. Provider breakdown accurate
5. Config section [integrations.rano] works
6. Graceful fallback when rano missing
7. Unit tests for detection and attribution
8. E2E test for full rano integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:13:42.503613234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:10:14.654483967Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-308f5","depends_on_id":"bd-99a2i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-30nv1","title":"Implement keystroke_echo probe method","description":"Implement keystroke_echo probe method.

## Method

Send invisible character, check for cursor/output response.

## Implementation

```go
func probeKeystrokeEcho(session string, pane int, timeout time.Duration) ProbeResult {
    // 1. Capture baseline (include cursor position if possible)
    baseline := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
    
    // 2. Send null byte (invisible, non-disruptive)
    exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "send-keys", "-t", target, "\x00").Run()
    
    // 3. Wait for response (poll with short intervals)
    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
        current := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
        if current != baseline {
            return ProbeResult{
                Responsive: true,
                LatencyMs: ...,
            }
        }
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
    }
    
    // 4. No change detected
    return ProbeResult{Responsive: false}
}
```

## Considerations

- Null byte may not echo in all shells
- Alternative: Send ESC sequence that triggers cursor report
- Consider tmux capture-pane -p vs -pC (with cursor)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:32:57.558570422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:44:38.018027499Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:44:38.017983126Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30nv1","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30nv1","depends_on_id":"bd-58w0z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30nv1","depends_on_id":"bd-ok7rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-30o1y","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign --clear assignment removal logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for ntm assign --clear functionality.

## Test Cases

### Single Clear
- Clear assigned bead
- Verify assignment removed from store
- Verify file reservations released

### Batch Clear
- Clear multiple beads (comma-separated)
- Verify all cleared correctly
- Partial failure handling

### Clear by Pane
- Clear all assignments for pane 3
- Handle pane with no assignments

### Clear Failed
- Clear only failed assignments
- Leave working/completed untouched

### Error Handling
- Clear non-assigned bead -> error
- Clear completed bead without --force -> error
- Clear completed bead with --force -> success

### Edge Cases
- Clear already cleared assignment
- Clear during concurrent assignment

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-30ws","title":"Task: Implement SummarizeSession() function","description":"## Purpose
Generate concise, actionable summaries of agent session output at session end.

## Scope
- Implement SummarizeSession() in internal/summary/generator.go
- Extract key accomplishments, changes made, and pending work
- Parse structured output from agent sessions
- Support multiple summary formats (brief, detailed, handoff)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summaries capture all significant changes
- File changes are listed with context
- Pending work items are extracted
- Error summary included if applicable
- Token count reasonable for injection into next session

## Technical Notes

- Fallback to pattern-based extraction
- Integrate with Agent Mail for thread summaries","notes":"## Testing Requirements
